journal_entry.description: Conversion of 50 preferred shares to 25 common shares
journal_entry.rationale: Under IFRS, share conversion is an equity-to-equity transaction that does not affect total equity. The carrying value of the preferred shares ($100) is transferred to common shares. Debit Preferred Shares (equity decrease, slot e=1) to remove the converted shares, and credit Common Shares (equity increase, slot b=1) to recognize the newly issued shares. No gain or loss is recognized on conversion as this is a capital transaction between equity classes.

output.reason: Conversion of preferred shares to common shares is an equity-to-equity transaction. The credit side decreases Preferred Share Capital (equity decrease) by debiting it, and increases Common Share Capital (equity increase) by crediting it. Since we only classify the CREDIT side, we record one line: Common Share Capital = equity increase.

credit_classifier.reason: Conversion of preferred shares to common shares is an equity-to-equity transaction. The credit side decreases Preferred Share Capital (equity decrease) by debiting it, and increases Common Share Capital (equity increase) by crediting it. Since we only classify the CREDIT side, we record one line: Common Share Capital = equity increase.

output.reason: Converting preferred shares to common shares is an equity reclassification transaction. The debit side decreases the Preferred Shares equity account (equity decrease), while the credit side will increase the Common Shares equity account. This is a pure equity-to-equity conversion with no impact on assets, liabilities, expenses, revenues, or dividends. Debit: 1 line for equity decrease (Preferred Shares).

debit_classifier.reason: Converting preferred shares to common shares is an equity reclassification transaction. The debit side decreases the Preferred Shares equity account (equity decrease), while the credit side will increase the Common Shares equity account. This is a pure equity-to-equity conversion with no impact on assets, liabilities, expenses, revenues, or dividends. Debit: 1 line for equity decrease (Preferred Shares).

All necessary lines present, amounts match transaction text, directionality correct.
